title: Blakea arboricola

The plant belongs to the genus Blakea, which consists of approximately 20 species.

intro: Blakea arboricola, also known as Blakea, is a plant species native to the Andean region, belonging to the family Melastomataceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which offer potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and combating infections. The bioactive constituents of Blakea arboricola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Blakea arboricola is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and sometimes as a tincture or extract, to harness its health benefits.
